title: Platanthera unalascensis
taxonomy: Platanthera unalascensis is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. The genus Platanthera, to which it belongs, is a group of terrestrial orchids commonly known as bog orchids or green orchids. Within the genus Platanthera, Platanthera unalascensis is a distinct species found in North America. It is further classified as a member of the subgenus Lepanthes, which includes several species of terrestrial orchids. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, including its flowers and leaf structure.
uses: Platanthera unalascensis, also known as Unalaska bog orchid, has been traditionally used by the indigenous peoples of the Pacific Northwest for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots and leaves are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin infections. In some traditional medicine practices, the plant is also used to treat fever and respiratory issues.
